beforeStartingEachStep.instruction: Before starting any step, check both referenced session summary files and confirm the current project state matches their completed and remaining work.

tasks: Create the admin-dashboard folder at the project root.
Create middleware that protects all /admin routes.
Read the x-admin-key header from each incoming request.
Compare the header value to process.env.ADMIN_API_KEY.
Call next() when the key matches.
Return 401 Unauthorized with a JSON error message when the key is missing or invalid.

requirements: Query all documents from the Url collection.
Support optional query param ?sort=clicks or ?sort=createdAt.
Default sorting should be createdAt descending.
Return 200 with an array of all URL documents.
Read code from req.params.
Find the document by shortCode.
Return 404 if not found.
Return 200 with the full document including originalUrl, shortCode, clicks, lastAccessed, createdAt, and customSlug.
Read code from req.params.
Find and delete the URL by shortCode.
Return 404 if not found.
Return 200 with { "message": "Deleted successfully" }.
Read code from req.params and originalUrl from req.body.
Validate the new originalUrl using the same validation as createShortUrl.
Find the URL by shortCode and update originalUrl.
Return 404 if not found.
Return 200 with the updated document.
Calculate totalUrls as the count of all documents.
Calculate totalClicks as the sum of all clicks fields.
Return topUrls as the top 5 documents sorted by clicks descending.
Return recentUrls as the last 5 documents sorted by createdAt descending.
Return 200 with all stats in one JSON object.

tasks: Apply adminAuth middleware to all routes in this file using router.use(adminAuth).
Add GET /admin/urls mapped to getAllUrls.
Add GET /admin/urls/:code/stats mapped to getUrlStats.
Add DELETE /admin/urls/:code mapped to deleteUrl.
Add PATCH /admin/urls/:code mapped to updateUrl.
Add GET /admin/stats mapped to getGlobalStats.
Enable cors middleware so the standalone admin dashboard can call the API from another origin or local file.
Mount admin routes at / before public routes so /:code does not swallow /admin/* routes.
Do not serve admin-dashboard through Express; keep the backend API-only.
Create a standalone static HTML file that communicates with the backend via fetch().
Use a dark utilitarian dashboard design with a table-based layout.
Add a top nav with Refresh and Logout buttons.
Define const API_BASE = "http://localhost:3000" at the top of the script block.
Use API_BASE for all fetch() calls.
Include the x-admin-key header in all API calls.
authenticationUi: On page load, check localStorage for a saved admin key under adminKey.
If no key is present, show a full-screen overlay with a key input and Unlock button.
On submit, store the key in localStorage and load the dashboard.
If any API call returns 401, clear localStorage and show the key prompt again.

sections.name: Global Stats
display: Most clicked URL with shortCode and click count

sections.name: All URLs Table
columns: Original URL truncated to 50 chars with full URL in title attribute
Last Accessed formatted date or Never
Actions with Edit and Delete buttons
deleteFlow: On Delete click, show confirm dialog: Delete this short URL? This cannot be undone.
If confirmed, send DELETE ${API_BASE}/admin/urls/:code with the admin key header.
On success, remove the row from the table without a full page reload.
editFlow: On Edit click, replace the Original URL cell with an input pre-filled with the current URL and a Save button.
On Save, send PATCH ${API_BASE}/admin/urls/:code with { originalUrl: newValue }.
On success, update the cell value and restore the display.

sections.name: URL Detail / Stats Modal
behavior: Clicking a short code in the table opens a modal.
Fetch detail stats for that short code.
Display full original URL, short code, total clicks, created at, and last accessed.

generalBehavior: Show a loading spinner while fetching data.
Show a top banner error message if any fetch fails.
Logout button clears localStorage and shows the key prompt.
During development, open admin-dashboard/index.html directly in a browser. No server is needed for this file.

tasks: ADMIN_API_KEY is read from backend/.env only and never hardcoded.
backend/.env is listed in .gitignore.
All /admin/* routes are protected by adminAuth middleware.
cors middleware is enabled in backend/server.js so the static admin dashboard can reach the API.
API_BASE is defined at the top of admin-dashboard/index.html and is not scattered across fetch() calls.
Admin key in the browser is stored in localStorage, acceptable only for a personal tool and not suitable for production multi-user scenarios.
Start the backend with npm run dev from the backend folder.
Verify GET /admin/urls returns data when x-admin-key is provided.
Verify GET /admin/stats returns global stats when x-admin-key is provided.
Verify DELETE /admin/urls/<code> deletes a URL when x-admin-key is provided.
Verify accessing /admin/urls without a key returns 401.
